jokes.text: You got a point behind all those teeth of yours?
jokes.setup: George Sr. trying to pitch his wall project with nervous rambling
jokes.punchline: Herbert Love cuts through the BS with a blunt insult about George Sr.'s appearance
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical putdown that establishes Herbert Love's direct personality while deflating George Sr.'s confidence

jokes.text: Action Jackson? What do I look like, Carl Weathers to you?
jokes.setup: George Sr. mentions 'action' in business context
jokes.punchline: Herbert Love's non-sequitur reference to Carl Weathers (who appeared in earlier seasons)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Arrested Development meta-humor that rewards longtime viewers with Carl Weathers callback

jokes.text: getting Barack Hussein Obama Wama Comma Mama on a Llama out of office
jokes.setup: Political candidate listing his platform points
jokes.punchline: Escalating nonsense rhyme that reduces Obama's name to gibberish
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating wordplay that captures political rhetoric's descent into nonsense

jokes.text: It's a crazy-town name.
jokes.setup: The absurd Obama name rhyme he just delivered
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to his own ridiculous creation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up that makes the previous absurdity even funnier

jokes.text: George Sr. had neither.
jokes.setup: Herbert Love wanting redheads and greenbacks
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact observation of George Sr.'s complete failure to meet criteria
jokes.explanation: Classic narrator economy - maximum comedic impact with minimal words

jokes.text: You want to reach your hand into my pocket to pay for your birth control? I get to put my hand in your pocket to put it in.
jokes.setup: Herbert Love explaining his political platform
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sexual metaphor about government spending
jokes.explanation: Accidental sexual innuendo in political rhetoric that characterizes Herbert Love's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: I had to duck a woman's pump and a mini pizza.
jokes.setup: Herbert Love describing negative reaction to his speech
jokes.punchline: Escalation from shoe to food being thrown, with specific absurd details
jokes.explanation: Specific absurd details (mini pizza) elevate a simple 'things thrown at politician' joke

jokes.text: And, although too explicit for an American audience, the lovemaking was intense and beautiful
jokes.setup: Narrator describing intimate scene
jokes.punchline: Show acknowledging its own censorship limitations
jokes.explanation: Self-aware commentary on broadcast standards that maintains the show's meta-textual humor

jokes.text: Turn him around three times and put him in Fullerton, he won't know the difference.
jokes.setup: Discussion about moving Buster somewhere
jokes.punchline: Treating adult son like a disoriented pet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille cruelty - specific, visual, and perfectly in character. The pet comparison is darkly brilliant.

jokes.text: Buster? No, no. I won't... I won't hear of it. It could kill him. I meant Gob.
jokes.setup: Oscar's protective reaction about sending 'their son' to dangerous work
jokes.punchline: Lucille clarifies she meant Gob, Oscar immediately changes tune
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection that reveals family dynamics - Oscar's favoritism and disregard for Gob

jokes.text: Didn't someone die in one of these things? / Oh, no, not in one of ours. / Although his pitch had lost some of its luster.
jokes.setup: George Sr. trying to sell potentially dangerous housing
jokes.punchline: Narrator's dry observation about his sales pitch quality
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about dangerous housing mixed with narrator's dry commentary. Well-structured.

jokes.text: And, you know, I mean, it's better than dying in the snow. I mean, not... not-not that you'll die, but if you had to choose, uh, I mean... Brr, you know?
jokes.setup: George Sr. trying to sell sweat lodge experience
jokes.punchline: Accidentally suggesting death, then making it worse with recovery attempts
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy - George Sr. digging deeper with each attempt to recover

jokes.text: Why, 'cause I'm black?
jokes.setup: George Sr.'s Republican strategist comment
jokes.punchline: Herbert Love calling out the obvious racial assumption
jokes.explanation: Direct, uncomfortable truth that punctures George Sr.'s casual racism

jokes.text: Well, I should have known you'd be here, supporting this right-wing, dangerous crackpot.
jokes.setup: Lindsay confronting George Sr. at Republican fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Her calling Herbert Love a crackpot while she's there supporting him too
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's political hypocrisy - attacking what she's also doing

jokes.text: Lindsay, I forgot-- you dyed your hair.
jokes.setup: Lindsay appearing with different hair
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s matter-of-fact observation as if just noticing
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag acknowledgment, sets up ongoing hair color confusion

jokes.text: Yeah, the first four months, I was in denial. How are you?
jokes.setup: George Sr. explaining why he didn't contact Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of months of denial as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s casual admission of psychological dysfunction delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: a $50,000 check your wife sent to my daughter for plastic surgery. What kind of a woman does that?
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining about Lucille sending money for plastic surgery
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's outrage about plastic surgery despite her own extensive work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay hypocrisy - condemning what she embodies

jokes.text: You don't think Maisie's cuter?
jokes.setup: Being corrected about Maeby's name
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on the wrong name as if it's a preference
jokes.explanation: Escalates the name confusion by suggesting his wrong version is actually better

jokes.text: And that's when George Sr. finally tipped a black man.
jokes.setup: George Sr. grabbing the check from the Black waiter
jokes.punchline: Narrator's double meaning - both 'gave a tip' and 'knocked over'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that works on multiple levels - both literal physical action and financial tip, with racial undertones

jokes.text: Oh, no, I-I'm a man. I was just wearing, uh, this blouse because I was, uh, driving and I hate my arms.
jokes.setup: Border agents addressing George Sr. as 'ma'am'
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s mortifying explanation for wearing women's clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - the explanation is worse than the situation. George Sr.'s vanity creates comedy gold.

jokes.text: Whoever told you that's probably chomping on the maca, if you know what I mean.
jokes.setup: Discussion about border location
jokes.punchline: Reference to maca root from earlier season (hallucinogenic properties)
jokes.explanation: Rewards long-time viewers with maca root callback, though delivery is less than stellar

jokes.text: You really hurt my feelings, guys.
jokes.setup: Border agents being official and demanding passport
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s inappropriate emotional response to bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s emotional fragility showing in inappropriate context

jokes.text: You can't expect me to read every page of that deal. Besides, that one was particularly difficult because it was all in Spanish.
jokes.setup: George Sr. being confronted about not knowing his property is in Mexico
jokes.punchline: Admitting he can't read Spanish despite buying Mexican land
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Sr. incompetence - buying property without understanding basic details

jokes.text: Se habla español. I do not speak Spanish.
jokes.setup: Business card in Spanish
jokes.punchline: Card saying opposite of what George Sr. thinks it says
jokes.explanation: Classic language mix-up that reveals George Sr.'s assumptions and incompetence

jokes.text: Well, this is America. If they don't want us to burn signs, they shouldn't write them in Spanish.
jokes.setup: Oscar burning Spanish signs
jokes.punchline: Twisted logic justifying destruction of property
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about xenophobia delivered with Oscar's oblivious confidence

jokes.text: Only Heartfire knew the truth. But the singing was so loud, no one could hear her thoughts.
jokes.setup: Heartfire trying to communicate truth about property location
jokes.punchline: Her thoughts being drowned out by singing, as if thoughts were audible
jokes.explanation: Absurdist narrator logic treating thoughts as if they were audible speech

jokes.text: It's a 40 for the flip and a 40 for the flop, and since it's going to cause a flap, I'd say another 40. So it's a flat 40-40-40 flip-flop and a flap fee.
jokes.setup: Lawyer explaining costs for Herbert Love to change position
jokes.punchline: Escalating alliteration turning political flip-flopping into nonsense fee structure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating wordplay that turns political terminology into absurd fee structure. Pure AD wordsmith excellence.

jokes.text: Hey, you've changed, you big (bleep). You're acting like a little girl lately. And not in the good way.
jokes.setup: George Sr. being emotional and weak
jokes.punchline: Lucille's characteristically harsh assessment with inappropriate qualifier
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille cruelty with the added transgressive 'not in the good way' qualifier

jokes.text: I'm not a limo driver. I'm a gentleman honey farmer. The only thing I've got back there are my bees. Beans? No, bees! Bees? Bees! Bees! Oh, bees!
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his profession and cargo
jokes.punchline: Communication breakdown over bees/beans leading to panic
jokes.explanation: Classic AD escalation with wordplay confusion leading to physical comedy setup

jokes.text: Colony collapse disorder. The guy sold me just really sick bees.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining why the bees won't listen
jokes.punchline: Real scientific term applied to Gob's incompetent bee management
jokes.explanation: Clever use of actual scientific terminology to explain Gob's magical incompetence

jokes.text: Don't worry. Dad, they're going to hive. They always hive.
jokes.setup: Dangerous bee situation
jokes.punchline: Gob's confident misuse of bee terminology
jokes.explanation: Gob's confident incompetence with bee terminology while bees are attacking people

jokes.text: Bees! Wonderful bees!
jokes.setup: Dangerous bee swarm attacking people
jokes.punchline: Crowd's inexplicably positive reaction to bee attack
jokes.explanation: Absurd positive reaction to dangerous situation. Simple but effective visual comedy.

jokes.text: Hello, brother. Thought I'd find you in a blouse and a beekeeper hat.
jokes.setup: Oscar arriving to find George Sr.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to George Sr.'s cross-dressing and current ridiculous situation
jokes.explanation: Oscar's casual acknowledgment of George Sr.'s absurd appearance choices

jokes.text: Oh, God, it was Buster's map.
jokes.setup: Learning the property border confusion came from Buster's map
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s horror at trusting Buster with important task
jokes.explanation: Perfect family dynamic - the realization that Buster was involved explains everything

jokes.text: Buster had indeed made the map, as a cartography student, because the class where you get to see naked people, and draw them, was full.
jokes.setup: Explaining Buster's cartography background
jokes.punchline: He only chose it because figure drawing (with nudity) was unavailable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster characterization - incompetent choices driven by inappropriate motivations

jokes.text: I told one lie, but I-I was going to share the money with you. Two lies. And why do I think that you charge more than five dollars for lemonade? Three small lies.
jokes.setup: George Sr. trying to minimize his lies
jokes.punchline: Each justification becomes another lie, perfectly counted by Oscar
jokes.explanation: Beautiful escalating structure where each excuse becomes evidence of more lies

jokes.text: There's two Dr. Normans!
jokes.setup: Client seeing both George Sr. and Oscar at sweat lodge
jokes.punchline: Confusion at seeing identical twins in same role
jokes.explanation: Classic twin confusion moment that creates immediate physical comedy

jokes.text: Yesterday, you were amazing. Georgie, I can't stop thinking about it. You haven't been like that in years.
jokes.setup: Lucille greeting George Sr. after Oscar's visit
jokes.punchline: Lucille praising 'George Sr.' for Oscar's performance in bed
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy from dramatic irony - audience knows George Sr. is being praised for his brother's performance

jokes.text: You mean, slow-witted?
jokes.setup: Lucille saying he was amazing yesterday
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s accidental self-roast
jokes.explanation: George Sr. accidentally admitting his own deficiencies while trying to understand the compliment

jokes.text: That's my penis.
jokes.setup: Doctor manipulating 'spot' that can create sexual feeling
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of obvious anatomical correction

jokes.text: Well, you don't have to tell me. Nothing?
jokes.setup: George Sr. pointing out doctor is touching his penis
jokes.punchline: Doctor's inappropriate familiarity with patient's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Awkward doctor-patient boundary crossing for uncomfortable humor

jokes.text: I mean, there's some violating federal law stuff that'd put me in prison for the rest of my life, but I've made love with treason charges over me before.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking about pressure affecting sexual performance
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s casual admission of federal crimes and sexual experience under legal duress
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Sr. character moment - treating federal crimes as minor stressors and referencing past sexual performance under legal pressure

jokes.text: But I'm the one who can get hard, so let's focus on you.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining homeopathy
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate assertion of sexual prowess to deflect from pseudoscience
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor. The inappropriate assertion deflecting from medical incompetence is well-timed.

jokes.text: Have you come across any ancient games, let's say, glowing games, uh, either board based or carnival based, that you may have played against the wishes of your best friend?
jokes.setup: Doctor running through supernatural medical possibilities
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific question referencing horror movie tropes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist medical questioning that parodies horror movie curse origins

jokes.text: The strong will become the weak. The weak will become the strong.
jokes.setup: George Sr. remembering hallucination from maca root
jokes.punchline: Vague mystical prophecy that actually explains current situation
jokes.explanation: Mystical prophecy that retrospectively explains the twin personality swap

jokes.text: I'd like you to get on an MRI machine and try to ride it to climax.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting medical imaging for George Sr.'s problem
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate medical procedure suggestion
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate medical advice that crosses professional boundaries for shock humor

jokes.text: And this whole car full of black kids ran over it, for no reason. And they saw it! They saw it!
jokes.setup: George Sr. explaining his emotional state through hat incident
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate racial specificity and emotional overreaction
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s emotional fragility expressed through inappropriate racial commentary and victim mentality

jokes.text: You can't swim in that bay for a month after without getting nacho cheese sauce in your hair.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Cinco de Cuatro celebration consequences
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd detail about cultural celebration aftermath
jokes.explanation: Absurd specific consequence of cultural celebration that creates vivid mental image

jokes.text: ♪ Happy birthday, Mr. President... ♪ I-I don't want to be Mr. President.
jokes.setup: Lucille doing Marilyn Monroe impression
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s rejection of the fantasy due to current political situation
jokes.explanation: Role-play fantasy interrupted by real-world political anxiety

jokes.text: I just want my son to have a job where his incompetence won't be out of place.
jokes.setup: George Sr. explaining why he wants Gob in movie business
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that insults both Gob and Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Perfect double insult - Gob's incompetence and Hollywood's low standards. Economical and brutal.

jokes.text: Actually that was a memorial to the death of Pete the mailman. I knew that.
jokes.setup: Gob claiming memorials represent death of housing market
jokes.punchline: Michael's correction and Gob's immediate lie about knowing
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob defensive lying when caught being pretentious

jokes.text: If I were Muslim, would you say 'Muslim magic act'?
jokes.setup: Michael calling it a 'Christian magic act'
jokes.punchline: Gob's false equivalency about religious discrimination
jokes.explanation: Gob's misunderstanding of discrimination while defending his ridiculous act

jokes.text: No, but it is hard to imagine any Muslim handcuffing himself to a glittery cross and living to tell about it.
jokes.setup: Gob's Muslim discrimination question
jokes.punchline: Michael's logical observation about religious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about religious conflicts with specific visual reference to Gob's act

jokes.text: Yeah, well, the Christians aren't so (bleep)-hot about it either.
jokes.setup: Michael pointing out danger of Christian magic act
jokes.punchline: Gob's casual admission that Christians also hate his act
jokes.explanation: Good Gob understatement. His casual acceptance of universal rejection is funny character work.

jokes.text: Like Bryce Dallas and Paige Carlyle, was named after where she was conceived.
jokes.setup: Explaining naming pattern of Ron Howard's children
jokes.punchline: All his children named after conception locations
jokes.explanation: Clever real-world reference humor. The pattern recognition is satisfying for viewers familiar with Ron Howard.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I'm dating Ron Howard's girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Michael discovering identity of his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about the show's actual producer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke referencing the show's actual executive producer Ron Howard

jokes.text: She was Ron's illegitimate daughter, a fact unbeknownst to many.
jokes.setup: Narrator describing Rebel's relationship to Ron Howard
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on the show's inside joke with 'unbeknownst'
jokes.explanation: Meta-textual humor with self-aware narrator acknowledging the show's own fictional conceits

jokes.text: You're thinking of Moe Howard, the angry stooge who's been dead for a quarter century.
jokes.setup: Gob confusing Ron Howard with Three Stooges
jokes.punchline: Michael's correction highlighting the absurdity of the confusion
jokes.explanation: Clean correction that highlights Gob's pop culture confusion while being informative

jokes.text: Daddy, my tummy's turning. I got to get out, I got to get out.
jokes.setup: Buster trying to act as military representative in promotional video
jokes.punchline: Buster's childish breakdown during important business presentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - adult man reverting to childhood during stress

jokes.text: No, thanks. I've already crushed one with my car.
jokes.setup: Someone offering George Sr. something at Cinco
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to vehicular violence
jokes.explanation: Dark callback to earlier hat incident with casual delivery

jokes.text: 'Cause I'm fat. It's because I am fat. You think I'm a fat person. These fat arms.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 saying she can't be seen with George Sr.
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s body dysmorphia projection
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s insecurity manifesting as body image issues in inappropriate context

jokes.text: your testosterone numbers are off the charts. Below the charts, under the charts. I don't know how to say that.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining test results
jokes.punchline: Escalating ways to describe extremely low numbers
jokes.explanation: Escalating medical terminology confusion that emphasizes how bad the results are

jokes.text: I think I dumped it with a pot pipe and a bottle of cheetah sedative.
jokes.setup: George Sr. asking to see his medical results
jokes.punchline: Doctor casually mentioning disposing of results with illegal/exotic items
jokes.explanation: Absurd list of items doctor casually mentions having, suggesting questionable medical practices

jokes.text: You, sir, are a very funny lady.
jokes.setup: George Sr.'s appearance and behavior during examination
jokes.punchline: Doctor's backhanded compliment about George Sr.'s feminized state
jokes.explanation: Backhanded compliment that acknowledges George Sr.'s hormonal transformation

jokes.text: This feels right.
jokes.setup: George Sr. putting on disguise/women's clothing
jokes.punchline: His comfortable acceptance of feminine presentation
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s gender identity confusion/acceptance moment

jokes.text: You're right, I mean, it's not like we're even technically related.
jokes.setup: Lindsay and Michael reunion after long absence
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's inappropriate suggestion about their relationship
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate suggestion based on their non-blood relationship

jokes.text: I don't allow men in my apartment without my husband present.
jokes.setup: Detective wanting to question George Sr.
jokes.punchline: George Sr. in drag claiming propriety while avoiding police
jokes.explanation: Perfect disguise comedy - George Sr. adopting feminine propriety to avoid police questioning
